options.a: A rare phenomenon involving wind shear; It is usually found in the lower levels of the troposphere and is associated with strong inversions and thunderstorms.
options.b: The erratic movement of air in the absence of any significant visual clues is caused by bodies of air moving at very different speeds.
options.c: Principally an invisible vertical wind shear formed only when adjacent air currents have significantly different directions.
options.d: The violent interaction of different airflows, and it is encountered only in the upper troposphere around jet streams.
